title: Certified Kubernetes Administrator (CKA)
note: The real CKA is a hands-on, performance-based exam (live cluster tasks), not multiple choice. These MCQ sets build conceptual recall and command knowledge — pair them with actual kubectl practice on a cluster.

questions.stem: A node shows NotReady. Which command first confirms whether the kubelet is the cause?

options.text: systemctl status kubelet on the affected node
options.explanation: Correct. NotReady usually traces to the kubelet; checking its service status on the node is the first diagnostic step.
Wrong. Deleting the node removes it from the API without diagnosing anything — destructive and premature.
Wrong. That restarts app pods, unrelated to node readiness.
Wrong. Draining evicts workloads; it's a remediation step, not a diagnosis, and shouldn't be first.

questions.stem: Which field keeps a pod from being scheduled onto a node unless the pod explicitly tolerates a condition the node advertises?

options.explanation: Correct. Taints repel pods that lack a matching toleration, controlling what may land on a node.
Wrong. nodeSelector is set on the pod, and it attracts rather than repels.
Wrong. Limits cap usage; they don't gate scheduling by node condition.
Wrong. PDBs constrain voluntary disruptions, not scheduling placement.

questions.stem: Which Service type exposes a pod on a static port on every node's IP?

options.explanation: Correct. NodePort opens the same port across all nodes, routing to the Service.
Wrong. ClusterIP is internal-only with no node port exposure.
Wrong. ExternalName maps to a DNS name via CNAME; no ports involved.
Wrong. A headless service (clusterIP: None) returns pod IPs directly; it doesn't expose a node port.

questions.stem: A PersistentVolumeClaim stays Pending. Which is the most likely cause?

options.text: No PV or StorageClass satisfies the claim's size/access mode
options.explanation: Correct. A PVC binds only when a matching PV exists or a StorageClass can dynamically provision one; otherwise it stays Pending.

options.text: The pod has too many replicas
options.explanation: Wrong. Replica count doesn't affect PVC binding.
Wrong. Network policies govern traffic, not volume binding.
Wrong. kube-proxy handles service networking, unrelated to PVC binding.

questions.stem: Which component is the cluster's source of truth, storing all API objects?

options.explanation: Correct. etcd is the consistent key-value store backing the entire cluster state.
Wrong. The scheduler places pods; it stores nothing persistently.
Wrong. The kubelet runs on nodes and manages pods locally; it isn't the state store.
Wrong. kube-proxy programs service networking rules, not state storage.

questions.stem: Which actions can safely free a node for maintenance? (Select TWO)

options.explanation: Correct. cordon marks the node unschedulable so no new pods land on it.
Correct. drain evicts pods (respecting PDBs) while skipping DaemonSet pods, readying the node for maintenance.
Wrong. Deleting removes the node object entirely — not a maintenance prep step.
Wrong. PreferNoSchedule is a soft hint, not a guarantee, so pods may still schedule there.
